q.en: What rumor about Del spread through the school?
q.zh: 学校里流传着关于戴尔的什么谣言？

options.en: He beat up a kindergartner and was expelled.
options.zh: 他打了一个幼儿园小朋友，还被开除了。

options.en: He won a school reading award.
options.zh: 他赢得了一个校内阅读奖项。

options.en: He moved to a new school far away.
options.zh: 他转去了很远的另一所学校。

options.en: He saved a younger student from danger.
options.zh: 他救了一个低年级的同学。

explain.en: Matt told Jamie the rumor said Principal Cosgrove expelled Del for beating up a kindergartner.
explain.zh: 马特告诉杰米，谣言说科斯格罗夫校长因为戴尔打了幼儿园小朋友而把他开除了。

q.en: Why was Del really in Principal Cosgrove's office?
q.zh: 戴尔到底为什么会在科斯格罗夫校长的办公室里？

options.en: His mom was telling him and his little brother their dad, hurt overseas in the Army, was coming home.
options.zh: 他妈妈正告诉他和小弟弟，他们在海外服役受伤的爸爸要回家了。

options.en: He was in trouble for bullying a classmate.
options.zh: 他因为欺负同学惹了麻烦。

options.en: He was receiving a special Student of the Month award.
options.zh: 他要去领一个“本月之星”特别奖。

options.en: He was registering for a brand-new school.
options.zh: 他正在办理转学手续。

explain.en: Del explained his mom and little brother were there; his dad had been hurt while deployed overseas and was being sent home.
explain.zh: 戴尔解释说当时是他妈妈和小弟弟在场，爸爸在海外服役时受伤，即将被送回家。

q.en: What does the 'gossip monster' in the story represent?
q.zh: 故事里的“八卦怪物”象征什么？

options.en: A rumor that grows and changes as it is passed from person to person.
options.zh: 一个在人们口中不断传播、变形并越来越大的谣言。

options.en: A real creature that lives in the school basement.
options.zh: 一只住在学校地下室里的真实怪物。

options.en: A pet that Del keeps at home.
options.zh: 戴尔在家养的一只宠物。

options.en: A noisy machine in the cafeteria.
options.zh: 食堂里一台很吵的机器。

explain.en: Mr. Sanchez compares starting or passing a rumor to feeding a monster that mutates and grows.
explain.zh: 桑切斯老师把散布谣言比作喂养一只会变异、会不断长大的怪物。

q.en: In Mr. Sanchez's Telephone game, what did the message become by the time it reached him?
q.zh: 在桑切斯老师的“传话游戏”里，这句话传到他耳朵里时变成了什么？

options.en: "Dope leaning ringing in your ear."
options.zh: “Dope leaning ringing in your ear（耳边叮当作响）”。

options.en: "Double leaves everything in your ear."
options.zh: “Double leaves everything in your ear（叶子落进你耳朵）”。

options.en: "Del beat up a kindergartner."
options.zh: “戴尔打了幼儿园小朋友”。

options.en: "Don't tell anyone the secret."
options.zh: “别把秘密告诉任何人”。

explain.en: The original sentence 'Don't believe everything that you hear' mutated into 'Dope leaning ringing in your ear.'
explain.zh: 原句“不要相信你听到的一切”在传递中变成了“Dope leaning ringing in your ear”。

q.en: What lesson does Mr. Sanchez want students to learn from the Telephone game?
q.zh: 桑切斯老师想让学生从传话游戏中明白什么道理？

options.en: Rumors change and grow as they spread, so you shouldn't believe every story you hear.
options.zh: 谣言在传播中会变形变大，所以不该轻信听到的每个说法。

options.en: Whispering is a good way to keep secrets safe.
options.zh: 小声耳语是保守秘密的好办法。

options.en: Science class is more fun than recess.
options.zh: 科学课比课间休息更有趣。

options.en: Only teachers should talk during class.
options.zh: 课堂上只有老师能说话。

explain.en: He says passing on a rumor is like feeding a monster and that words have consequences.
explain.zh: 他说散播谣言像喂养怪物，并且言语是有后果的。

q.en: Why did the author most likely write The Gossip Monster?
q.zh: 作者写《八卦怪物》最可能的目的是什么？

options.en: To show how gossip can hurt people and encourage readers to think before spreading rumors.
options.zh: 为了说明八卦会伤害他人，并鼓励读者在传播谣言前三思。

options.en: To teach readers how to play kickball.
options.zh: 为了教读者怎么玩踢球游戏。

options.en: To describe an exciting school field trip.
options.zh: 为了描写一次刺激的校外郊游。

options.en: To explain how to win a science fair.
options.zh: 为了讲解怎样在科学展上获奖。

explain.en: The story follows Jamie learning that her gossip hurt Del, highlighting the harm of rumors.
explain.zh: 故事通过杰米意识到自己的八卦伤害了戴尔，凸显了谣言的危害。

q.en: How did Jamie feel after she apologized to Del?
q.zh: 杰米向戴尔道歉后感觉如何？

options.en: Better and almost smiling, since Del used her real name for the first time in months.
options.zh: 轻松了一些，几乎露出微笑，因为戴尔几个月来第一次叫了她的真名。

options.en: Angry that Del ignored her apology.
options.zh: 因为戴尔不理她的道歉而很生气。

options.en: Proud that she had started the rumor.
options.zh: 为自己发起谣言感到骄傲。

options.en: Sad that she had to leave school.
options.zh: 因为要离校而感到难过。

explain.en: Del called her 'Jamie' for the first time in months, and she 'almost smiled.'
explain.zh: 戴尔几个月来第一次叫她“杰米”，她“几乎笑了起来”。

q.en: What made Jamie realize that the rumor about Del was wrong?
q.zh: 是什么让杰米意识到关于戴尔的谣言是错的？

options.en: Mr. Sanchez's Telephone game showed how rumors mutate and grow, plus her own guilt.
options.zh: 桑切斯老师的传话游戏展示了谣言如何变异变大，加上她自己的愧疚。

options.en: A television news report about the school.
options.zh: 电视上对这所学校的报道。

options.en: A note delivered by Del's mother.
options.zh: 戴尔妈妈送来的一张纸条。

options.en: Winning a prize in science class.
options.zh: 在科学课上得了一个奖。

explain.en: The game sparked the idea in her head, leading her to confess and seek the truth.
explain.zh: 传话游戏启发了她，促使她坦白并去寻求真相。

q.en: What advice does Mr. Sanchez give about gossiping?
q.zh: 桑切斯老师关于八卦给出了什么建议？

options.en: Don't gossip about people when they're not around unless you're saying something nice.
options.zh: 别人不在场时不要说闲话，除非你说的是好话。

options.en: Always repeat rumors so everyone stays informed.
options.zh: 一定要把谣言传开，这样大家都能知情。

options.en: Only talk about teachers, never about students.
options.zh: 只议论老师，别议论同学。

options.en: Rumors are harmless fun and should be ignored.
options.zh: 谣言只是无害的玩笑，不必理会。

explain.en: He says let people speak for themselves and don't gossip unless it is kind.
explain.zh: 他说要让当事人自己说话，除非说好话，否则不要背后议论。

q.en: Why did Del call Jamie 'Germy' instead of her real name?
q.zh: 戴尔为什么叫杰米“Germy”而不是她的真名？

options.en: A rumor said Jamie got sick from crawling through garbage, and the nickname stuck.
options.zh: 有谣言说杰米因为钻垃圾桶才生病，这个外号就一直留了下来。

options.en: Jamie loved collecting germs for a science project.
options.zh: 杰米喜欢为科学课收集细菌。

options.en: Her middle name was actually Germaine.
options.zh: 她的中间名其实是Germaine。

options.en: Del simply could not pronounce 'Jamie.'
options.zh: 戴尔就是不会发“Jamie”这个音。

explain.en: A summer rumor claimed Jamie got sick by crawling through garbage, leading to the nickname.
explain.zh: 去年夏天有谣言说杰米钻垃圾桶才生病，于是有了这个绰号。

prompt.en: Have you ever heard a rumor that turned out to be untrue? What could you do to stop a gossip monster from growing?
prompt.zh: 你有没有听过后来发现并不真实的谣言？你可以做些什么来阻止“八卦怪物”越变越大？

en: Think about a time you heard or passed along a story that changed as it traveled.
Consider one kind action, like asking the source or speaking up, that could help.
zh: 回想一下你曾听到或转述过、后来却变样的故事。
想想一个善意的做法，比如去问当事人或站出来说明，会有什么帮助。
